source: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — Chapter III (High-risk) and Chapter IV (Transparency)

PROHIBITED.requirement: Cease development and deployment. The AI system falls under one of the prohibited practices listed in Article 5 and cannot be placed on the Union market, put into service, or used.
Conduct a formal legal review to confirm the prohibition finding and document the discontinuation decision with dated records.

HIGH_RISK.requirement: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system running throughout the entire lifecycle of the AI system.
HIGH_RISK.deadline: Before placing on the market — by 2 August 2026

HIGH_RISK.requirement: Apply data governance practices to training, validation and testing datasets: relevance, representativeness, freedom from errors, and appropriate statistical properties. Examine for biases affecting health equity.
Draw up technical documentation demonstrating conformity with the requirements of Chapter III, Section 2. Must be kept up to date.
Enable automatic recording of events (logs) over the duration of the system lifetime to ensure traceability.
Design for sufficient transparency: provide clear instructions for use enabling deployers to interpret outputs appropriately in the clinical context.
Design for effective human oversight. In clinical contexts: ensure clinicians can override, interpret, and refuse AI outputs without automation bias.
Achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity; document performance metrics and validation methodology.
Establish a Quality Management System (QMS). Where the provider is already subject to ISO 13485 (MDR/IVDR), integration is permitted.
Undergo the applicable conformity assessment procedure. For SaMD (MDR/IVDR), this is typically performed by a Notified Body and combined with the existing medical device conformity assessment.
Affix the CE marking and draw up the EU declaration of conformity before placing the system on the market.
Register the high-risk AI system in the EU database before placing it on the market or putting it into service.
Establish a post-market monitoring system to collect, document and analyse performance data throughout the lifetime of the system.
Report serious incidents to the market surveillance authority without undue delay, and in any case within the deadlines specified.
HIGH_RISK.deadline: Ongoing — within 15 days (or 2/10 days for serious incidents involving death or irreversible disruption)

HIGH_RISK.requirement: Where the deployer is a public authority or entity providing public services, conduct a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) before deployment.

LIMITED_RISK.requirement: If the system interacts directly with natural persons (e.g. patient-facing chatbot), inform them they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from context.
LIMITED_RISK.deadline: Before placing on the market — by 2 August 2026

LIMITED_RISK.requirement: If the system generates synthetic audio, image, video or text content, mark outputs as artificially generated or manipulated in a machine-readable format.
For deepfakes, clearly disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
Maintain internal documentation of classification rationale and transparency measures applied. Recommended even though not strictly mandatory for limited-risk systems.

MINIMAL_RISK.requirement: Consider voluntary adherence to codes of conduct promoting the application of high-risk requirements (e.g. transparency, human oversight, bias mitigation) on a voluntary basis.
Ensure compliance with GDPR for any processing of personal or health data, regardless of AI Act classification.
Monitor intended purpose and deployment context. If substantial modification alters risk profile, reassess classification.
